title: UntrimFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of an Untrim feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setLoops
methods.description: Set the loops to be removed.

methods.name: setLoopsFromFaces
methods.description: Set the loops to be removed from a set of faces.

properties.name: extensionDistance
properties.description: Gets and sets the ValueInput object that defines the extension distance applied to faces when an external boundary is removed.

properties.name: facesToUntrim
properties.description: Gets the face objects to untrim. Returns null/None in the case where loops are specified instead of faces.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: loopsToUntrim
properties.description: Gets the loop objects to untrim. Returns null/None in the case where faces are specified instead of loops

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: untrimLoopType
properties.description: Gets the loop type to be untrimmed. This is only used when faces are being untrimmed and is ignored for loops.

related_samples.name: Untrim Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new untrim feature.

classType.title: UntrimFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setLoops.title: UntrimFeatureInput.setLoops Method
setLoops.description: Set the loops to be removed.
setLoops.return_description: Returns whether the operation was successful

parameters.name: loops
parameters.description: Redefines this input to remove loops from the body. If faces were previously defined, that information will be lost. Only loops that do not have a connected face can be removed (the edges in the loop have a single face) The array can only contain loops from surface bodies, (the isSolid property of the BRepBody returns false).

setLoopsFromFaces.title: UntrimFeatureInput.setLoopsFromFaces Method
setLoopsFromFaces.description: Set the loops to be removed from a set of faces.
setLoopsFromFaces.return_description: Returns whether the operation was successful

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: An array of BRepFace objects that will have the loops of the specified types removed. Only loops that do not have a connected face can be removed (the edges in the loop have a single face). The array can only contain faces from surface bodies, (the isSolid property of the BRepBody returns false).

parameters.name: untrimLoopType
parameters.description: The loop type to be untrimmed (AllLoopUntrimType, InternalLoopUntrimType, or ExternalLoopUntrimType).

extensionDistance.title: UntrimFeatureInput.extensionDistance Property
extensionDistance.description: Gets and sets the ValueInput object that defines the extension distance applied to faces when an external boundary is removed.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = untrimFeatureInput_var.extensionDistance

# Set the value of the property.
untrimFeatureInput_var.extensionDistance = propertyValue

facesToUntrim.title: UntrimFeatureInput.facesToUntrim Property
facesToUntrim.description: Gets the face objects to untrim. Returns null/None in the case where loops are specified instead of faces.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = untrimFeatureInput_var.facesToUntrim

loopsToUntrim.description: Gets the loop objects to untrim. Returns null/None in the case where faces are specified instead of loops

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

untrimLoopType.description: Gets the loop type to be untrimmed. This is only used when faces are being untrimmed and is ignored for loops.
